feed.title: SAP Community - Artificial Intelligence
feed.subtitle: Artificial Intelligence blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Artificial Intelligence in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Welcome to your one-stop shop to learn and share knowledge about SAP's ready-to-run public cloud ERP software solution.   

SAP Cloud ERP is part of SAP Business Suite that brings AI, data, and applications together into a fully integrated system. This community is open for anyone in the cloud ERP ecosystem–customers, partners, and SAP experts.  

In this blog, I’m thrilled to guide you through the latest AI innovations in our SAP Cloud ERP release 2602. This release marks a significant leap forward in our journey to embed reliable, relevant, and responsible AI directly into the core of your ERP. Our goal is simple: to make your work smarter, your decisions faster, and your daily tasks more intuitive.

With our AI co-pilot Joule and a host of new embedded AI capabilities, we're transforming the user experience and delivering tangible business value across every line of business. Let's dive into the highlights!

A Smarter Foundation: Cross-Topic AI Innovations

We're embedding AI into the very fabric of the user experience, making the system more proactive, helpful, and personalized for every user.

1. Situation handling

1.1. AI-assisted situation handling (release 2602.1)

Solve business challenges faster and smarter with AI-powered recommendations:

  • Instant, Personalized Guidance: Get tailored advice right when you need it, helping you resolve business issues quickly and confidently.
  • Flexible and Customizable: Adapt the solution to your unique business logic—make changes easily and keep your processes up to date.
  • Effortless Access to Knowledge: Find the information you need, even beyond SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, with simplified, contextual support.

Key Features:

  • Generate actionable recommendations based on your specific business context.
  • Create and manage prompts to ensure solutions are relevant and effective.
  • Enrich recommendations with data from multiple sources for deeper insights.

With AI-Assisted Situation Handling, you empower your team to work smarter, make better decisions, and stay agile in a fast-changing business environment.

For more information, see: Situation Handling

Discovery Center

 

2. User experience

2.1. AI-assisted error explanation for SAP Fiori elements-based Applications

With AI-assisted error explanations for SAP Fiori elements-based applications, you’ll save valuable time, boost reliability, and make maintenance easier than ever. Faster resolution of errors with the support of generative AI:

  • Speed Up Maintenance: Let generative AI quickly explain errors and recommend solutions, so you spend less time fixing issues and more time driving your business forward.
  • Reduce Future Errors: Understand the root causes behind problems, helping you prevent similar issues from happening again.

For more information, see: Discovery Center

 

2.2. AI-assisted smart personalization of My Home for applications

Tailor My Home experience using natural language and transform your workspace with just a few words:

  • Natural Language Personalization: Simply tell SAP what you need—AI understands your requests and instantly suggests the most relevant apps for your daily tasks.
  • Quick and Easy Setup: Add new applications to your “My Home” in seconds. Preview AI-generated suggestions before confirming, so you always get what fits best.
  • Save Time, Boost Productivity: No more searching through menus. AI streamlines your workflow, making it ideal for both new and occasional users.
  • Trustworthy Recommendations: Rely on intelligent, data-driven suggestions that ensure accuracy and build confidence in your setup.
  • Seamless Experience: Enjoy a smooth, click-minimizing process with a preview-based confirmation—making personalization simple and enjoyable.

Unlock the full potential of SAP Cloud ERP by letting AI tailor your “My Home” to your needs—so you can focus on what matters most

For more information, see: Discovery Center

 

2.3. AI-assisted easy fill of SAP Fiori elements-based applications

Let AI handle the details—just describe what you need, and watch your forms fill themselves:

  • Save Time Instantly: Maintain all the fields on your object pages in seconds, thanks to seamless AI-powered automation.
  • No More Guesswork: Simply use natural language, and the system intelligently matches your words to the right fields—no more hunting through forms.

With AI-assisted easy fill, you’ll breeze through data entry, reduce manual effort, and focus on what matters most in your business.

For more information, see: Discovery Center

 

2.4. AI-assisted task automation (beta)

AI-assisted task automation is the next embedded AI feature in the SAP Fiori user experience of SAP Cloud ERP. It builds on the previously delivered features such as easy filter, easy fill and smart solutions for situations in My Home.​

It allows key users and end users to automate their daily activities in SAP’s Fiori apps by using generative AI to create so called “smart helpers”. Those ”smart helpers” execute the repetitive activities on behalf of the end user either from the SAP Fiori launchpad or from individual SAP Fiori apps while always keeping the user in the loop for decisions on data changes.​

Benefits:

  • Easy to use SAP Fiori application to create “smart helpers”​
  • Full control of what the “smart helper” proposes to do​
  • Intuitive way of running the “smart helper” while being always in control in case of changes​
  • Supports all SAP Fiori elements-based applications​

Key features:

  • Creation of “smart helpers” based on all available SAP Fiori elements-based applications in SAP Cloud ERP​
  • Generation and validation of execution plan for “smart helpers”​
  • Run time for “smart helpers” is integrated into My Home the product homepage of SAP Cloud ERP​

Please register to be part of the SAP Beta Testing of the Newest AI Innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

2.5. AI-assisted smart solution for situations (beta)

AI-assisted smart solution for situations in My Home simplifies workflows by dynamically adapting the user interface to guide business users through resolution steps using the power of generative AI

Directly on the SAP Cloud ERP homepage, users can resolve operational situations in just a few clicks. AI-driven recommendations accelerate decision-making and streamline user actions for improved efficiency

Benefits:

  • Drives time efficiency by eliminating navigation through intelligent UI design
  • Enhances decision-making with timely, context-aware AI recommendations
  • Delivers trustworthy AI-generated results, built on enterprise-grade intelligence
  • Offers an intuitive, click-efficient experience, empowering users to act confidently with minimal effort.

Key features:

  • Key information and actions are surfaced through dynamic cards reducing the need for in-depth navigation
  • Enables one-click resolution of situations directly from the homepage thereby enhancing operational efficiency within SAP Cloud ERP
  • Situation types supported:
  • End date for project reached but stage not completed
  • Alert for bank messages
  • Expiring Sales Quotations

Please register to be part of the SAP Beta Testing of the Newest AI Innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

AI Innovations Across Lines of Business:

3. Finance

3.1. Accounting Accruals agent (beta)

Close Your Books Faster with the AI-Powered Accounting Accruals Agent

The agent helps accountants to automate journal entry preparation for accruals by analyzing historical data and leveraging accounting policy documents. It calculates accurate amounts and provides a pre-populated list of journal entries for review and confirmation. Your accounts will increase their productivity, ensure timely month-end close, and get more time for high-value activities.

  • Automate Journal Entries: Let AI handle the heavy lifting by preparing accrual journal entries for you—twice as fast as before.
  • Boost Accuracy: The agent analyzes your historical data and accounting policies to calculate precise accrual amounts, reducing errors and manual work.
  • Ready-to-Review Entries: Receive a pre-populated list of journal entries, so your team can quickly review and confirm, saving valuable time.
  • Focus on What Matters: Free up your accountants to concentrate on high-value activities, ensuring a timely and efficient month-end close.

Business Impact:

  • Cut time spent on accrual calculations and deferrals by up to 80%
  • Reduce review and posting time by 50%
  • Achieve a faster, smoother period-end close

For more information, see: Discovery Center

 

3.2. International trade classification agent (beta)

Classify your products for international shipping in seconds—no expertise required

This agent helps product classification specialists to classify goods for shipping. The agent reasons over product characteristics and trade regulations and acts to classify goods for international shipping. The agent recommends customs tariff numbers and commodity codes, providing rationale for easy review and approval.

This agent will help ensure compliance with global trade regulations and reduce the risk of manual errors with robust governance and a clear, audit-ready decision

  • Automatic Classification: Let AI analyze your product details and trade regulations to instantly recommend the right customs tariff numbers and commodity codes.
  • Ensure Compliance: Rely on recommendations that are reasoned from both product characteristics and up-to-date trade rules, helping you meet global regulations with confidence.
  • Reduce Risk: Eliminate manual errors with a robust, auditable decision process—every classification comes with clear rationale for easy review and approval.

Business Impact:

  • 50% reduction in effort to classify international trade products

For more information, see: Discovery Center

 

3.3. Sales accounting: period comparisons and business insights for Product Profitability Review Booklet

The Product Profitability Review Booklet enables you, as a Sales Accountant, to analyze the profitability for products sold using built-in business pages for the Margin Overview and the Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Breakdown.

Benefits:

  • Gain deeper insights into margins and cost of goods sold (COGS) with period comparisons
  • Enable informed decision-making with AI-enabled financial business insights
  • Support flexible market segment analysis with an extensible CDS view
  • Improve visibility with additional currency types and dimensions

Key features:

Enhancements to the Product Profitability Review Booklet app that:

  • Provide new period comparisons for product profitability
  • Use AI to generate financial business insights into period comparisons for product profitability
  • Add a commenting feature
  • Display results in functional currency
  • Enable CDS view to support extensibility for market segment
  • Incorporate additional dimensions and navigation targets

For more information, see: Enhancements in the Product Profitability Review Booklet.

 

3.4. SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity: Using Joule to access solution-related content 

Have a question about SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity? Whether you’re curious about features, setup steps, or integration scenarios, Joule is here to help! With Joule, you can simply ask your question in natural language—no technical jargon needed. Joule instantly searches the SAP Help Portal, analyzes the latest resources, and delivers clear, context-aware answers tailored to your needs.

Benefits:

  • Ask Naturally, Get Answers Instantly: Express your business needs in your own words and receive quick, relevant guidance.
  • Faster Self-Service: Get crisp, summarized content so you can resolve issues or learn new features without digging through documentation.
  • Save Time: Joule streamlines your search, helping you find the right information faster so you can focus on your work.

Key features:

  • Smart Search & Summaries: Joule scans SAP Help Portal documentation and summarizes the most important details about SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity processes and features.
  • Easy Deep Dives: Need more detail? Joule provides direct links to the original documentation for further exploration.

For more information, see: Joule with SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity | SAP Help Portal

 

3.5. SAP Green Ledger: Joule for reporting: AI-assisted carbon emission analysis (09/2025)

Instantly Visualize and Understand Your Carbon Data with Joule in SAP Green Ledger

Joule in Green Ledger can generate charts from carbon data through conversational prompts, providing automatic visualizations for reporting purposes. This tool is useful for efficiently analyzing and presenting carbon data in a clear and intuitive format.

Benefits:

  • Actionable Insights for Sustainability: Instantly see trends and breakdowns in your greenhouse gas emissions, empowering you to make smarter decisions for carbon reduction and long-term success.
  • Save Time, Focus on Strategy: Automated reporting means less manual work and more time for impactful analysis and decision-making.
  • Effortless for Everyone: The intuitive interface makes advanced carbon reporting accessible to all users, regardless of technical background.

Key features:

  • Tailored Visualizations: Generate column, donut, and line charts from carbon data, customized to your business needs and reporting requirements.
  • Support for Multiple Dimensions: Analyze data by GHG category, GHG scope, fiscal year, and more, with carbon emissions as the primary measure. 
  • Smart Context Handling: Joule understands follow-up questions, so you can keep exploring your data without starting over

For more information, see SAP Green Ledger, carbon emission analysis in SAP Discovery Center.

 

3.6. SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: AI-assisted trend analysis and summary for reports (beta)

The Trend Analysis feature in Joule provides natural language interaction to help you analyze VAT returns. You can ask Joule to generate comparative trend reports and detect outliers. Joule delivers insights that make VAT reporting faster, more accurate, and easier to understand.

Joule can also summarize the generated reports, helping you review key trends and results quickly. This makes it easier to identify anomalies and support faster approvals.

Benefits:

  • Accelerates VAT review and approval cycles: Natural-language trend analysis and auto-summarized insights reduce the time required to interpret VAT data across periods, enabling faster reviews, quicker approvals, and shorter reporting cycles.
  • Improves accuracy and reduces compliance risk: Automated detection of trends, fluctuations, and outliers helps identify anomalies early, minimizing manual errors and lowering the risk of incorrect filings or regulatory non-compliance.
  • Enables informed, data-driven decision-making: Clear, comparable trend reports and concise summaries transform complex VAT data into actionable insights, helping finance and compliance teams make confident decisions with less effort.

Key features:

  • Use Joule to generate a trend analysis for a VAT return across multiple reporting periods. The analysis highlights key trends, fluctuations, and irregularities, and flags values that deviate from expected norms to help you identify potential errors or compliance risks.
  • Joule generates a concise summary of key VAT insights to support faster reviews, approvals, and decision-making.

 

3.7. SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, Cloud Edition: intelligent alert handling and investigation recommendations

Investigations are the central entities to examine security incidents in SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition. Intelligent recommendations automatically add case-specific response and mitigation recommendations to investigations based on predefined value lists and machine learning functionality. 

Benefits:

  • Decrease manual effort and increase efficiency for SAP monitoring agents

Key features:

  • Automated Recommendations: The system uses predefined value lists and machine learning to generate tailored recommendations for each investigation.
  • Guided Investigation Preparation: SAP monitoring agents are supported with automatic recommendations when compiling investigation results and suggestions for customers.
  • Standardized Response Generation: Recommendations are based on standard operating procedures for the detected alert patterns, ensuring consistency and reliability in incident response

For more information, see: Generating Investigation Recommendations

 

Sales

4.1. Automatic creation of sales orders from unstructured data (premium)

Internal sales representatives can create sales orders from purchase order files in PDF or image formats (unstructured data). After a file is uploaded, the system automatically extracts file information into a sales order request and proposes values for sales order request fields (for example, by determining the sold-to party based on extracted data). The sales order request can later be converted into a sales order.

Benefits:

  • Increase sales force efficiency through the automatic creation of sales orders from unstructured data
  • Reduce manual effort

Key features:

  • Automatic Data Extraction: Instantly extract and populate sales order details from unstructured files like PDFs and images
  • Intelligent Field Suggestions: The system proposes values for sales order fields, such as the sold-to party, based on the extracted data.
  • Multilanguage Support: SAP Document AI enables extraction and training in multiple languages, making the solution accessible for global teams.

For more information, see: Create sales orders from unstructured data

 

Procurement

5.1. Conversational search for local and central purchase requisition status (Release 2510)

Designed for casual users, who easily and quickly want to inform themselves about the status of their purchase requisition, the intelligent Joule assistant can be queried to fetch the follow-on document status of a requestor’s self-service purchase requisition

Benefits:

  • Provide quicker and easier access to the purchase requisition and follow-on document status

Key features:

  • Query Joule for the approval and follow-on document status of a given local or central purchase requisition without the need to navigate into the details of another SAP Fiori app
  • Enter any of the related document numbers—for example, local or central requisition number, purchase requisition number in the connected system, or cart number in SAP Ariba—when asking for the document status

 

Asset Management

6.1. Maintenance notification creation and screening with Joule (release 2602)

SAP Cloud ERP 2602 makes asset management easier for everyone.

Now, you can quickly create maintenance notifications just by telling Joule—using simple, everyday language—what the issue is, which equipment it affects, and how urgent it is. All your notifications are managed in the new SAP Fiori app, where you can easily follow up and turn them into maintenance orders with just a few clicks. This update makes reporting problems faster, reduces downtime, and takes the hassle out of routine maintenance tasks.

Benefits:

  • Simplified Maintenance Processes: Create maintenance notifications quickly and easily using natural language, reducing the complexity of traditional workflows
  • Faster Fault and Incident Response: Accelerate the reporting and handling of maintenance issues, enabling quicker resolution and less downtime.
  • Increased Efficiency: Streamline operations and boost productivity by leveraging AI-driven automation for routine asset management tasks.

 

Key features:

  • AI-Assisted Notification Creation: Use Joule to generate maintenance notifications by simply providing key details (description, technical object, priority) in natural language.
  • Integration with SAP Fiori App: Access and manage created notifications directly in the new SAP Fiori app “Manage Maintenance Notification (F5777)”
  • Conversational Order Creation: Accept requests and convert notifications into maintenance orders within a single conversational context.
  • Natural Language Processing: Perform operations and interact with the system using everyday language, making the process faster and more accessible.

For more information, see: What’s new for Joule – SAP Help Portal

 

Manufacturing

7.1. AI-assisted production routing proposal and copy routing (release 2602)

Save time and reduce errors by letting AI do the heavy lifting! Now, you can quickly generate new production routings by reusing existing ones—no more repetitive data entry.

Benefits:

  • Streamlined Process: Production engineers, planners, and master data specialists can easily create and edit routings, ensuring consistency and accuracy every time.

Key features: 

  • Use AI to find similar routing based on material name, description, and bill of material to propose close matches
  • Get recommendations of routing that you can copy or reuse, based on similarity
  • Copy and edit the proposed routing to arrive at the new routing

​As a prerequisite, you need to enable the related ISLM scenario. For information on setting up the ISLM scenario, see this blog post. For general information on ISLM, see: Intelligent Scenario Management.

For more information, see:

  • Intelligent Scenario to Find Similar Materials for Routing Proposal
  • Create a Routing using the AI-Assisted Material Search

 

7.2. Manage production orders - Production Planning and Operations agent (beta)

With this feature, you can start the Production Planning and Operations agent from the worklist of the Manage Production Orders app. The app now includes the Joule Release button that enables you to release your production orders assisted by agentic AI technology.

Benefits:

  • Reduce manual work with automated validation of material, capacity, and scheduling availability
  • Keep production moving with recommended workarounds for material and scheduling conflicts
  • Boost production throughput by cutting order-processing delays and minimizing downtime between order releases

Key features:

  • Assisting the production planners and supervisors in releasing up to 20 production orders at a time.
  • The agent automates prerequisite checks necessary for releasing production orders, including component availability and alternatives as well as work center capacity checks.
  • The agent also proposes rescheduling when the prerequisites for release are not met.
  • Once all criteria are met, the planner can confirm the proposed order changes, and the agent will complete the production order release.

For more information, see: Manage Production Orders - Production Planning and Operations Agent

Discovery Center

 

Joule For Consultants

 

Clearer Source Citations
SAP Joule for Consultants now makes it easier to see where information comes from. Citations are more visible and, when relevant, include results from public web searches. This gives you a transparent view of the sources behind every answer, helping you trust and verify the information you receive.

 

Expanded Reference Data
The SAP Enterprise Architecture Reference Library (EARL) is now partially available in Joule conversations. As more EARL content is added, you’ll see even richer and more accurate answers, with relevant references included right in your responses.

 

SAP Joule for Consultants Console

The SAP Joule for Consultants Console now offers new tools for both standard users and administrators. You can access the latest SAP release notes, usage statistics (for admins), a built-in Prompt Library, and system settings—all in one place

 

Access to Knowledge Base Articles and SAP Notes
Joule for Consultants now includes Knowledge Based Articles (KBA) and SAP Notes. If your company’s service agreement covers these, you’ll have access automatically—no extra steps needed for end-users. Administrators just need to make sure the right email addresses are assigned.

 

 

New Private Content

  • Simplification List: Stay up to date with changes in SAP S/4HANA. The Simplification List shows which features have changed, been removed, or replaced, so you can plan and adapt smoothly.
  • IMG (Implementation Guide): Easily find documentation and step-by-step activities to configure SAP functions for your organization, using a clear, structured navigation.

Quick Links for Faster Access
The “Show Prompt Guide” button is now “Quick Links.” This gives you instant access to the Console, Prompt Guide, and Prompt Library—all with a single click.

 

Longer Conversations Supported
You can now enter up to 10,000 characters in a single conversation—five times more than before. This is especially helpful for sharing longer code snippets or detailed questions.

 

Richer, Refreshed SAP Public Content
Joule for Consultants has updated and expanded its public SAP content. You’ll now find more detailed information and a wider range of insights from SAP Learning, SAP Help, SAP News, the AI Feature Catalogue, and more

 

For more information, see: What’s New for SAP Joule for Consultants

Discovery Center

 

The Future is Intelligent

The 2602 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud demonstrates our commitment to building an ERP that is not just a system of record, but a true partner in your success. By embedding AI across the suite, we are helping you unlock new levels of productivity, enhance decision-making, and create a more intuitive and enjoyable user experience for everyone.

This is just the beginning, and I can't wait to see how you leverage these new capabilities to drive your business forward.

 

📽️ Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series! 

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Artificial Intelligence as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

 

🔎 Find all our assets 

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Generating and Integrating Automated Predictive Library (APL) Forecasts in a Seamless Planning Model
entry.content:
Introduction 

SAP Analytics Cloud has always been the one solution for BI, planning and predictive analytics. As such, it has powerful built-in capabilities for regression, classification and time-series forecasting. You know them as Predictive Scenarios and many of you have used them to support their planning processes. Our predictive scenarios are perfect for business users: they choose the best available algorithm for your data and explain the results while maintaining the semantics of the model throughout the process (e.g., hierarchies). With SAP Business Data Cloud and seamless planning, data scientists on the other hand can now leverage HANA’s predictive Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) and Automated Predictive Library (APL) directly on the HANA database of SAP Datasphere and nicely integrate the results into planning processes. This lets them tweak predictive models by picking and choosing the algorithm of their choice and use code instead of a UI. SAP BDC would also allow them to share data with SAP Databricks or another Databricks instance using data products if this was their preferred environment. 

This blogpost was created with @marc_daniau, a development expert for our predictive engine. We want to demonstrate the usage of HANA APL in combination with a seamless planning model and live versions. We do this using a straight-forward prediction based on actual data.  

 

High-level overview 

This is what we are working with: 

In SAP Analytics Cloud, we have a planning model deployed to an SAP Datasphere space. That makes it a seamless planning model which means that its data is not stored on the SAP Analytics Cloud database but only on the SAP Datasphere database.  

In the SAP Datasphere space, we find the planning model data and a table with actuals. We are not using an SAP BDC data product, but you surely could! 

We create our prediction directly on the underlying HANA Cloud database of SAP Datasphere. We created a DB user to access the database. There, we consume the actuals, create a stored procedure which we can trigger via a task chain, and surface the result in a view in the space. This fact data can then be added to the seamless planning model as a live version.  

 

Step-by-step 

1. Seamless planning model 

We do not cover the full creation and set-up of the model. Let’s just check out its key characteristics:  

  • It is a seamless planning model, deployed to the space Sales Planning Demo 
  • Its fact table is exposed in the SAP Datasphere space (actually, this is not decisive for the use case described here but could be useful if you want to add budget data as influence for your predictive model, for instance) 
  •  We want to create a forecast version and predict the measure SALES_REVENUE along the product and region dimension 

2. SAP Datasphere space

Again, we do not look at the creation of the space and all its artefacts. The following Actuals view is key as we extrapolate our forecast based on this data: 

  • Measures and attributes nicely match the planning model structure (which is handy for our simple demo scenario J) 
  • We excluded columns that we do not need in a projection 
  • We filtered on date as we do not want to use the entire history 

3. Setting up database access 

We are now ready to learn how to create the forecast on the HANA database. First of all, we need to set up database access.  

  • Prerequisite: Enable the SAP HANA Cloud Script Server on Your SAP Datasphere Tenant 
  • Navigate to Space Management, find your space and Edit.  
  • Navigate to Database Access and create a new user 
     
  • Name your user and make the needed settings as highlighted. Your user’s name will be a concatenation of your space name, ‘#’ and the suffix you provide here. 
     
  • Mark your user and open the database explorer. The password can be retrieved in the details of the user (information symbol).  

4. Database explorer 

Let’s first have an overview of what we are creating in the database explorer: 

 

  • We create a view that consumes the actual data from our space schema. Note the following: 
  • We concatenate Product and Region to one Entity column that we will use to segment our prediction. As we work directly on flat fact tables/views, we do not have the luxury of keeping all semantics as we have it in the SAP Analytics Cloud predictive scenarios.  

 

CREATE VIEW "SALES_PLANNING_DEMO#AI_USER"."APL_SERIES_IN" ( "Entity", "Date", "SalesRevenue" ) AS (select   
  "Product" || '|' || "Regions" as "Entity", "Date", "SalesRevenue" 
 from  
  SALES_PLANNING_DEMO."V_Actual_Sales_Data" 
 order by 1, 2)

  • We now create the prediction task as a stored procedure.  
create procedure "APL_FORECAST_TASK" 
as BEGIN
    declare header "SAP_PA_APL"."sap.pa.apl.base::BASE.T.FUNCTION_HEADER";
    declare config "SAP_PA_APL"."sap.pa.apl.base::BASE.T.OPERATION_CONFIG_DETAILED";
	declare var_desc "SAP_PA_APL"."sap.pa.apl.base::BASE.T.VARIABLE_DESC_OID";   
    declare var_role "SAP_PA_APL"."sap.pa.apl.base::BASE.T.VARIABLE_ROLES_WITH_COMPOSITES_OID";    
    declare apl_log   "SAP_PA_APL"."sap.pa.apl.base::BASE.T.OPERATION_LOG";      
    declare apl_sum   "SAP_PA_APL"."sap.pa.apl.base::BASE.T.SUMMARY";   
    declare apl_indic   "SAP_PA_APL"."sap.pa.apl.base::BASE.T.INDICATORS";   	
    declare apl_metr "SAP_PA_APL"."sap.pa.apl.base::BASE.T.DEBRIEF_METRIC_OID";
    declare apl_prop "SAP_PA_APL"."sap.pa.apl.base::BASE.T.DEBRIEF_PROPERTY_OID";      

    truncate table "SALES_PLANNING_DEMO#AI_USER"."APL_SERIES_OUT";
    truncate table "SALES_PLANNING_DEMO#AI_USER"."APL_FORECAST_ACCURACY";
    truncate table "SALES_PLANNING_DEMO#AI_USER"."APL_FORECAST_STATUS";
	
    :header.insert(('Oid', 'DSP APL'));
    :header.insert(('LogLevel', '2'));
    :header.insert(('MaxTasks', '4'));  --  PARALLEL TASKS

    :config.insert(('APL/SegmentColumnName', 'Entity',null)); 
    :config.insert(('APL/Horizon', '12',null));
    :config.insert(('APL/TimePointColumnName', 'Date',null));
	:config.insert(('APL/ForcePositiveForecast', 'true',null));
	:config.insert(('APL/DecomposeInfluencers', 'true',null));
	:config.insert(('APL/ApplyExtraMode', 'First Forecast with Stable Components and Residues and Error Bars',null));
	
    :var_role.insert(('Date', 'input', null, null, null));
    :var_role.insert(('SalesRevenue', 'target', null, null, null));
		
    "SAP_PA_APL"."sap.pa.apl.base::FORECAST_AND_DEBRIEF" (
	:header, :config, :var_desc, :var_role, 
	'SALES_PLANNING_DEMO#AI_USER', 'APL_SERIES_IN', 
	'SALES_PLANNING_DEMO#AI_USER', 'APL_SERIES_OUT', apl_log, apl_sum, apl_indic, apl_metr, apl_prop);

    insert into "SALES_PLANNING_DEMO#AI_USER"."APL_FORECAST_ACCURACY" 
    select "Oid" as "Entity", "MAE", "MAPE"
    from "SAP_PA_APL"."sap.pa.apl.debrief.report::TimeSeries_Performance" (:apl_prop, :apl_metr)
    where "Partition" = 'Validation';
	
	insert into "SALES_PLANNING_DEMO#AI_USER"."APL_FORECAST_STATUS" 
    select "OID" as "Entity", "VALUE" as "Task Status"
	from :apl_sum 
	where key = 'AplTaskStatus';
END

For our demo scenario we use the default APL forecasting method that automatically tries different hypotheses for trend, cycles and fluctuations, and eventually selects the combination that gives the best accuracy. For a faster processing on many segments, an option is to force the Exponential Smoothing method by adding to the procedure this line of code below: 

:config.insert(('APL/ForecastMethod','ExponentialSmoothing',null));

This is the code to prepare the target tables of the procedure: 

drop table APL_SERIES_OUT;
create table APL_SERIES_OUT (
 "Entity"  nvarchar(180),
 "Date" 	DATE,
 "SalesRevenue" DOUBLE,
 "kts_1" DOUBLE,
 "kts_1Trend" DOUBLE,
 "kts_1Cycles" DOUBLE,
 "kts_1_lowerlimit_95%" DOUBLE,
 "kts_1_upperlimit_95%" DOUBLE,
 "kts_1ExtraPreds" DOUBLE,
 "kts_1Fluctuations" DOUBLE,
 "kts_1Residues" DOUBLE
);

 

drop table APL_FORECAST_ACCURACY;
create table APL_FORECAST_ACCURACY (
 "Entity"  nvarchar(180),
 "MAE" DOUBLE,
 "MAPE" DOUBLE
);

 

drop table APL_FORECAST_STATUS;
create table APL_FORECAST_STATUS (
 "Entity"  nvarchar(180),
 "Task Status" nvarchar(180)
);
  • You can trigger this procedure manually using the command below: 
call "APL_FORECAST_TASK" ;

However, in the next chapter, we will also create a task chain in SAP Datasphere to trigger it which can be embedded in real workflows.  

  • You see we generate and write data into three different tables: 
  • The prediction result goes into our results table called APL_SERIES_OUT. This is the data that we want for our seamless planning model. The table has the concatenated entity, date and the predicted revenue. It also comes with upper and lower limit predictions (95%) as well as with fluctuations, extra predictions etc.  
     
  • Optionally, we create the table APL_FORECAST_ACCURACY to store the MAPE (Mean Absolute Percentage Error MAE (Mean Absolute Error) per entity. You could filter on the entities you are interested in, the best/worst entities etc. or you could get the MSE (Mean Squared Error) or RMSE (Root Mean Absolute Squared Error) as well.  

  • Optionally, we create the table APL_FORECAST_STATUS where we log the prediction status per entity. All our entities were successful.  

 

5. Task chain 

Stored procedures can be executed via task chains. As such, you can execute them from the SAP Datasphere UI, schedule them or trigger them via external API. Check out the task chain documentation to learn more about prerequisites such as required roles. Soon, you should be able to trigger this API via multi actions in SAP Analytics Cloud as well.  

We must allow the execution of the stored procedure via the SAP Datasphere UI including the creation and deletion of data in the database user schema:  

CALL "DWC_GLOBAL"."GRANT_PRIVILEGE_TO_SPACE" ( 
             OPERATION => 'GRANT',  
             PRIVILEGE => 'INSERT',  
             SCHEMA_NAME => 'SALES_PLANNING_DEMO#AI_USER',  
             OBJECT_NAME => '',  
             SPACE_ID => 'SALES_PLANNING_DEMO');

              

CALL "DWC_GLOBAL"."GRANT_PRIVILEGE_TO_SPACE" ( 
             OPERATION => 'GRANT',  
             PRIVILEGE => 'DELETE',  
             SCHEMA_NAME => 'SALES_PLANNING_DEMO#AI_USER',  
             OBJECT_NAME => '',  
             SPACE_ID => 'SALES_PLANNING_DEMO');

 

CALL "DWC_GLOBAL"."GRANT_PRIVILEGE_TO_SPACE" ( 
             OPERATION => 'GRANT',  
             PRIVILEGE => 'EXECUTE',  
             SCHEMA_NAME => 'SALES_PLANNING_DEMO#AI_USER',  
             OBJECT_NAME => '',  
             SPACE_ID => 'SALES_PLANNING_DEMO');

Setting up task chains is simple. To add a stored procedures, you select Others and browse through the available procedures that are available for your space. Then you drag the procedure on to the canvas to add it as a task.    

You can add replication flows, transformation flows, intelligent lookups etc. to your task chains. By that, you could for instance first refresh actuals and get them into shape so you can use them for your prediction. Or, you add an email notification task to receive updates after the execution of the task chain (I did that in the example below).  

6. Consuming results in the SAP Datasphere space 

Now that we have the predictive logic to calculate and we can execute it via the SAP Datasphere UI, we of course need to consume the forecast data in the planning model. To do that, we first need the results in the SAP Datasphere space. We create a view on top of the results table. We used a graphical view but depending on your preferences and skills, you may use an SQL view instead.  

  • Pull the results table from the DB user schema in the Sources tab. 

  • Add a calculation node to split the Entity column into regions and products again. Create two calculated columns (Product and Region) and use string functions.  The functions SUBSTR_BEFORE() and SUBSTR_AFTER() can be used to split a string using the first occurence of a specified pattern (in our case '|'  as the format of our Entity column is Product|Region). 

 Expression to derive product:

SUBSTR_BEFORE(Entity,'|')

Expression to derive region:

SUBSTR_AFTER(Entity,'|')
  •  Join the standard time dimension with day granularity to derive the calendar month from the date. Standard time tables and dimensions can be generated in Space Management (link).  

  • Add a projection and only keep the columns you need in the planning model. 
    • Calendar Month 
    • SalesRevenue 
    • Product 
    • Region 
  • Make sure to expose the view for consumption and select Fact as the Semantic Usage Type.  
  • Name the view and deploy.  

7. Adding the forecast result as live version in the seamless planning model 

We now move to SAP Analytics Cloud and add the forecast result to the seamless planning model as a live version.  

  • Connect external data source. 

  • Create a version to map the data into (or use an existing un-used version). 
  • Select the view. 
  • Map the columns. 
     
  • Preview the data. You see that we have a live connection to the forecast results in SAP Datasphere. So every time that the forecast is updated, it will be reflected in the planning model in real time! 

 

What (else) can/could you do with it?  

In SAP Analytics Cloud:  

  • You can display the live version data in the model data foundation and in tables, charts, etc. in stories. 
  • You can reference the live version data in model and story calculations as well as data actions (incl. advanced formulas). 
  • You cannot write back into the referenced views (or rather their underlying tables). But, you can copy the live version data into planning versions via copy/paste in the table, data actions and version management. 

In SAP Datasphere: 

  • You can of course leverage the forecast result in your views and analytic models and compare it to actuals, budgets, etc.  
  • You can run further transformations and calculations and report on the results or use them in planning.  
  • … 

 

Which new features can improve the workflow in the future? 

We are working on a couple of features that can make this scenario even better: 

  • Push data from SAP Datasphere into the seamless planning model via task chains:  
    Live versions are awesome. But, sometimes you want to use the prediction as a proposal and then edit it. You could copy the live version data into an editable version easily (see above) but with a push of data into the planning model, you could directly bring the data into an editable forecast version. That push could be nicely added to the same task chain that triggers the APL procedure. 
  • Trigger task chains from SAP Analytics Cloud:
    Task chains shall soon offer a public API for triggering task chain runs from outside of SAP Datasphere.
    We are getting ready on the SAP Analytics Cloud side to let you call this API via API steps in multi actions. With that, you could trigger the stored procedures from SAP Analytics Cloud.  
    Some day, we may have dedicated task chain steps in multi actions to ease this cross-orchestration. We also want to enable cross-orchestration in the opposite direction.  

 

Conclusion 

In this blogpost, Marc and I demonstrated how to integrate predictive forecast results from SAP HANA APL in a seamless planning model. We combined the power of SAP Datasphere, SAP HANA and SAP Analytics Cloud to achieve that in a quite straight-forward architecture. You could achieve more complex scenarios, use PAL instead to tweak your prediction more etc. Or you could use data products from SAP BDC to get you started even quicker.  

We are looking forward to the future enhancements that shall improve such workflows and the overall integration of planning into SAP BDC! 

 

Learn More 

  • Seamless Planning - Product FAQ 
  • Seamless Planning – Live Versions 
  • SAP HANA Automated Predictive Library (APL) 
  • SAP HANA Predictive Analysis Library (PAL)  
  • SAP Analytics Cloud Predictive Scenarios

entry.title: UKISUG & SAP Customer Support Day: AI-Powered Support that Reaches New Horizons
entry.content:
Dreams are not the only things without limitations. At UKISUG & SAP Customer Support Day, we’ll showcase how AI and its many possibilities pave the way for business outcomes where the sky is just another marker to reach, not a limit.

In the session “How SAP Drives Measurable Results with AI”, we’ll explore how Agentic Case Resolution (ACR) is being designed to make customer support smarter, faster, and more seamless. During the session, we’ll take a closer look at ACR’s use of intelligent automation to simplify case handling. Instead of manual steps and delays, ACR brings together advanced AI capabilities to streamline the support process from start to finish. You’ll see how this approach reduces resolution time, improves consistency, and frees teams to focus on delivering exceptional service and better customer experiences.

The path to creating these experiences continues through an AI‑powered support experience across SAP’s cloud solution landscape. By evolving its support strategy from reactive assistance to an Empowered, Assisted, and Invisible model that is highly personalized, proactive, and data‑driven, SAP, together with its customers, can turn impactful business results into reality. In the session “Seamless Support Across the Overall SAP Business Suite”, we’ll highlight the role of SAP for Me along this journey.

Positioned as the harmonized, end‑to‑end entry point for support, SAP for Me aims to enable faster problem resolution, richer customer empowerment, and prescriptive guidance throughout the customer journey. Through capabilities such as Generative AI Search and AI‑powered Assisted Support, SAP enhances self‑service and knowledge discovery while delivering personalized, context‑aware access through conversational AI.

If you’d like to learn more about AI possibilities across SAP’s support ecosystem, join us at UKISUG & SAP Customer Support Day  on February 26 and take the lead in turning AI potential into business outcomes.

entry.title: SAP Functional Consultants (OTC, P2P, etc.) in the Era of AI Agents - Things might change...
entry.content:
Traditional Functional Testing in SAP

SAP Functional Consultants (in areas like Order-to-Cash (OTC) or Procure-to-Pay (P2P)) have historically focused on configuring business processes and validating them via SAP’s user interfaces (SAP GUI or Fiori apps). Testing of integrated business processes was largely manual and deterministic – for a given input and configuration, the expected output was known. Common practices included:

  • Manual Process Execution: Executing transactions step-by-step in SAP (e.g. creating a Sales Order or Purchase Order) and verifying the outcome on the UI.
  • Using Transaction WE19 for IDocs: If a process involved EDI messages or IDocs, consultants might use WE19 (IDoc test tool) to simulate inbound or outbound IDoc processing. This allowed testing of interfaces that start with an IDoc, but only for classic EDI scenarios.
  • Relying on Integration Teams: For more complex integrations (like web services or APIs), functional consultants often depended on technical teams or waited for external partners to send test messages. Without specialized tools, they had no easy way to simulate those external calls themselves. As a result, testing cycles could be slow and dependent on others. Functional consultants typically viewed advanced automation tools as out-of-scope or overly technical – treating them “like mythical dragons, complicated, dangerous, and likely to drag them away from their real work”. This mindset led to minimal adoption of test automation by functional teams, keeping many tests manual and time-consuming.

The Shift to Integrated and AI-Driven Processes

Today’s SAP landscapes are increasingly integrated and intelligent. Several trends are transforming how business processes run and, consequently, how they must be tested:

  • Proliferation of APIs and Services: Many business processes now involve web services, REST/OData APIs, and cloud integrations (SAP Integration Suite). For example, an order might be created via an e-commerce API call, or a procurement process might involve a cloud portal sending data into S/4HANA via OData. These are not easily simulated with old tools like WE19 (which only works for IDocs).
  • Event-Driven and Cross-System Flows: End-to-end processes span multiple systems (SAP and non-SAP). A single workflow (e.g. Order-to-Cash) might trigger events in warehouses, tax portals, banks, or partner systems. Testing such flows (using for example SAP Advanced Event Mesh technologies) means ensuring each interface and handoff works – something beyond the scope of a single SAP GUI transaction.
  • Introduction of SAP AI Agents (e.g. SAP Joule): SAP is embedding Agentic AI into its applications. Agents like SAP Joule can autonomously perform tasks when given a goal or input (for instance, reading an incoming email with a purchase order PDF and then creating a Sales Order, Delivery, and Invoice in S/4HANA without human clicks). Instead of a user navigating SAP screens, the AI agent orchestrates the process. This is powerful, but it changes the nature of testing: you may need to test the agent’s behavior and the end results, rather than a user interface.

These changes bring a major challenge: the “death of determinism” in testing. With static workflows, one could predict “Input X via transaction Y will produce output Z” every time. An AI-driven process, however, might not follow the exact same path each run – the agent could take different actions or sequence of steps to achieve the same goal, and in worst cases might even “hallucinate” successes. For example, the agent might respond, “Order created successfully!”

Figure 1 – Order created – assuming you trust me

with a polite confirmation, but due to a hidden error (like a mis-mapped field), the order was never actually saved in the database. Traditional test cases that expect a fixed outcome can miss such issues, creating a verification gap where the system’s UI or the agent’s message says everything is fine while the database tells another story. In short, just because the AI agent says it did something, doesn’t guarantee the SAP system state changed accordingly.

Why Traditional Testing Methods Fall Short

Given the above shifts, the old ways of testing have clear limitations:

  • Inability to Simulate New Triggers: Tools like WE19 work for IDoc-based interfaces, but many modern processes begin with different triggers (HTTP calls, cloud events, emails to AI agents). Without the ability to simulate these, functional consultants cannot fully test the process on their own. For example, you can’t use WE19 to simulate a REST API call or an incoming email – you’d need a tool or script to mimic those external inputs.
  • Manual UI Testing Doesn’t Cover Backend Integration: A consultant testing via SAP GUI might verify that if they manually create an order, the subsequent IDoc or output is correct. But what if the order is created by an AI agent or an external system call? You’d have to initiate that external trigger and then verify the outcome inside SAP. Manually, this is cumbersome – it might involve sending a request via an API client (like Postman) or waiting for the AI to act, then checking multiple SAP tables or transactions to ensure everything posted correctly. This goes beyond the traditional comfort zone of many functional testers.
  • Non-Deterministic AI Behavior: As mentioned, an AI agent may not behave exactly the same way each time. Traditional test scripts that expect step-by-step consistency may fail even though the end result is acceptable, or worse, may pass a conversational check while missing a functional failure. Most AI governance tools on the market (so-called “guardrail” platforms) focus on prompt safety, security, and compliance – ensuring the AI doesn’t produce toxic output or leak data. They usually do not validate functional integrity of what the AI did. In other words, they watch what the agent says or intends (the tool calls), but not what actually happened in SAP S/4HANA system. If an AI agent politely confirms an action that failed in the backend, a guardrail or conversation test won’t catch that. This is a huge blind spot for enterprise scenarios.

In summary, the combination of more complex integration points and AI-driven logic means traditional, manual testing by clicking through SAP or using limited tools is no longer sufficient. Organizations risk missing critical issues if they don’t adopt new testing approaches. The question is: how will the SAP functional consultant’s role evolve to address this?

Evolving Role of the Functional Consultant: From GUI Tester to End-to-End Process Validator/Guardian 

Moving forward, SAP functional consultants in OTC, P2P, and other domains will likely see their responsibilities broaden. They will still configure business processes, but they will also need to ensure those processes work across system boundaries and with AI in the loop. Key aspects of this evolution include:

  • Testing Beyond the SAP GUI: Consultants will spend less time on purely manual UI testing and more on end-to-end scenario testing. For an order process, this might mean triggering the process the same way it would happen in real life (e.g. via an incoming EDI message, an API call, or an AI agent prompt) and then verifying the results across the system. The focus shifts from “Did the screen show the right data?” to “Did the entire workflow execute correctly in the backend systems?”.
  • Becoming API & Integration Savvy: While they may not become full developers, functional consultants will need a working understanding of how to simulate or call APIs, web services, and other integration points. For instance, a P2P consultant might use a tool to send a test JSON payload to an OData service that creates a vendor invoice, then check if the invoice posted and the approval workflow started. In essence, they take on some duties of an API tester, validating that the service calls and data mappings yield the expected results in SAP. This skillset ensures that even if a process is initiated outside of SAP, the consultant can still test it end-to-end.
  • Validating AI Agent Outcomes: As AI agents (like SAP Joule) handle tasks that consultants used to do via the UI, consultants will act as quality guardians for these agents. They might orchestrate test “conversations” or inputs for the agent and then verify the outcomes. For example, an OTC consultant may provide a test email (with an order PDF) to the SAP Joule agent and then confirm that: a Sales Order was created with correct data, a Delivery was generated, and an Invoice posted – all according to the business rules. Rather than checking a UI, they’ll check SAP backend records, document flow, or even database entries to ensure the agent’s actions are functionally correct. In other words, the consultant becomes a "human in the loop" for AI validation, focusing on what the AI did in the system rather than what the AI said.
  • Defining Success Criteria for Probabilistic Processes: In deterministic testing, success is binary (the output matches expected or not). With AI and complex integrations, success may need to be defined in terms of acceptable outcomes or system states. Functional consultants will be instrumental in defining test case expectations for AI-driven processes: e.g., “An order should be created with the correct customer and items, the tax should be calculated as per configuration, and no error IDocs should result.” They will design test cases that verify these conditions, even if the exact path the AI agent took (which transactions, what sequence) is not pre-scripted. This is sometimes referred to as a Functional Workflow Execution (FWE) validation – ensuring the workflow’s end results meet functional requirements, regardless of how the agent executed it.
  • Collaboration with Technical Teams: As their role expands into what was traditionally technical testing, functional consultants will likely work more closely with integration experts, BASIS, and QA automation engineers. They’ll need to understand logs, interface monitors, and possibly AI logs to troubleshoot when a test fails. However, rather than waiting for those teams to do the tests, the consultant will be empowered to run many tests themselves, escalating to experts only when something truly goes wrong at a technical level. This collaboration ensures that business process knowledge (which the functional consultant has) is combined with technical validation – a necessity for complex scenarios.

In essence, functional consultants are becoming end-to-end business process testers, ensuring that “if X leaves SAP or comes into SAP, it is exactly what the outside world expects,” even when X is triggered by an AI or external system. They will adopt some of the mindset and tools of AI testers and API testers, but with a focus on business correctness and usability rather than pure technical details.

The Role of Specialized Testing Tools (Int4 Suite and Similar)

Crucially, this evolution doesn’t mean every functional consultant must learn programming or become an automation engineer. New smart testing tools are emerging to bridge the gap, allowing functional consultants to test complex scenarios with minimal coding. One example is Int4 Suite, which has been highlighted as an “enterprise AI validator” or “secret shopper” for SAP processes. Tools like Int4 Suite offer features that directly address the needs we discussed:

  • Simulation of External Systems (Service Virtualization): Int4 Suite provides simulation agents that can stand in for external partners or systems during testing. For instance, a consultant can simulate an EDI partner sending an ORDERS message or a third-party warehouse sending a confirmation, without waiting for the real system. The tool leverages real production data – consultants can select actual historical messages (IDocs, API payloads, etc.) and replay them in the test environment on-demand. This means even if a process normally begins outside SAP, the functional tester can trigger it at will. It works for both EDI/IDoc and non-EDI (API/web service) interactions.
  • Simple Interface, No Coding Required: Instead of forcing consultants to write scripts or learn complex test frameworks, the tool presents a user-friendly interface (e.g., pick a historical message, tweak some values, and send). As one source put it, “The better path is not to force functional consultants into scripting or automation frameworks, but to give them simulation agents that mimic the system environment”. This approach avoids a steep learning curve. Consultants can use their SAP process knowledge to choose relevant test cases, and the tool handles the technical message sending and data injection behind the scenes.
  • End-to-End Validation (Deep Backend Checks): A critical feature is automatic result validation. When the consultant triggers a test (whether it’s creating a doc in SAP or sending an inbound message), Int4 Suite will automatically check the outcomes at a granular level. For example, after an outbound IDoc is generated or an API call is processed, the tool compares the new output to the expected output (often using the historical production message as the “gold standard”). It can validate that every relevant field matches, that totals are correctly calculated, and even that follow-on documents (like an invoice or accounting document) are created as expected. This is exactly the kind of functional integrity check that would catch an AI agent’s mistakes – if the agent missed a field mapping or made an error, the discrepancy would be flagged when comparing the new system output to the known correct output from a similar past scenario.

 Figure 2 – Backend Validation in Int4 Suite

 

  • “Secret Shopper” Testing for AI Agents: In the context of SAP’s AI agents, a tool like this can act as a secret shopper – essentially a fake customer that tests the agent. For the earlier example of an email-to-order scenario, Int4 Suite can automatically retrieve a real email and PDF order from the company’s archive, modify certain details to make it a unique test, and send it to the AI agent. Once the AI agent processes it (creating an order, delivery, invoice), Int4 Suite dives into S/4HANA’s backend to verify each document’s presence and correctness (e.g., checking that net value, tax, shipping point, etc., match the expected outcome). This way, the agent’s entire end-to-end functionality is validated, not just the message it returned in the chat. If something failed (like the agent said “order created” but no order exists, or the invoice values are wrong), the tool will catch it immediately.

 

Figure 3 – secret shopper figure

  • Use of Historical Data and Dynamic Data Fabrication: Because these tools often integrate with SAP directly, they can pull realistic test data. Int4 Suite’s approach is to use a “robotic crawler” to fetch historical messages and then dynamically fabricate variants of them for testing. This ensures test scenarios are very close to real business cases (with all the complexity of real data), increasing the chance of catching issues. Consultants can test edge cases (like a huge Black Friday order load, or a partially delivered order response) by simply replaying those real scenarios in a controlled way. This capability is far more powerful than hand-crafting test data in a spreadsheet.

By equipping functional consultants with such simulation and validation agents, organizations get several benefits: “Test cycles shorten dramatically, reliance on external partners and scarce integration resources drops, and confidence in end-to-end quality rises.” In other words, a tool like Int4 Suite transforms the functional consultant into a self-sufficient end-to-end tester, without turning them into programmers. They can rapidly run through many integrated scenarios on their own, as frequently as needed, which is essential in an AI-driven, non-deterministic world. It fills in the missing piece of testing by confirming that what leaves or enters SAP in these integrated processes is correct to the last field.

Possible Adaptation Strategies for Consultants

The transition to this new reality won’t happen overnight for everyone. Different organizations and individuals might take different approaches as these changes loom:

  • Full Embrace of the New Role: Many functional consultants (or entire teams) will fully embrace the role of AI/API-enabled process testers. They will upskill on using tools like Int4 Suite or other SAP testing frameworks and become comfortable simulating APIs and AI-driven scenarios. In this mode, a consultant effectively becomes a hybrid of a functional expert and an integration tester. They design test cases, use simulation tools to execute them, and interpret the results. This path ensures they remain highly relevant and valuable, as they can guarantee that business processes work correctly across all layers. It does require learning new tools and thinking beyond just SAP’s UI, but the payoff is a much faster and more reliable testing cycle – and fewer production surprises. (This is the “yes in all of those” changes scenario – taking on AI testing, API testing, and new tools altogether.)
  • Remain SAP-Centric (Traditionalist Approach): Some may choose (or initially attempt) to stick narrowly to classic SAP customization and UI testing, leaving the AI and integration aspects to specialized testers or developers. For instance, a consultant might test only the SAP transaction portions and assume that the integration team will ensure external inputs/outputs are correct. This “just SAP” approach might feel comfortable, but it has risks. It can create bottlenecks (always waiting on someone else for the end-to-end test) and blind spots (the consultant might miss how a configuration change impacts the API or agent behavior). In the long run, as SAP agents and integrations become core to processes, a consultant who doesn’t engage with those aspects could find their testing incomplete or their role limited. This option might work in the short term in organizations where roles are strictly separated, but it’s likely not sustainable as the norm shifts toward cross-functional testing skills.
  • Partial or Gradual Adoption: A middle path is to incrementally adopt these new responsibilities. Consultants might start by learning to use one simulation tool for a specific pain point (say, simulating an EDI partner message) while still doing other tests manually. Or they might pair up with integration engineers for a while – the consultant defines the business scenarios and expected results, and the engineer helps set up the API calls or agent triggers. Over time, the consultant picks up more of the skills and tools. In this “yes, but not too much” scenario, they do expand their role, but carefully and not all at once. This can be practical for teams that need to maintain current projects while gradually modernizing their testing approach. The key is that even partial adoption of tools like Int4 Suite can yield significant improvements (for example, using it just to simulate inbound messages can save a lot of time). Eventually, as confidence and competence grow, consultants can take on more.
  • New Specialist Roles Emerge: It’s also possible that entirely new roles or job titles will appear – for example, “SAP AI Process Tester” or “Enterprise AI Validation Engineer.” These could be professionals who bridge the gap between functional knowledge and AI technical know-how. Experienced functional consultants might transition into such roles, or they might work alongside them. In either case, the existence of such roles underlines that testing integrated, AI-infused processes is a specialty of its own. Consultants should be prepared to collaborate with these specialists or become one themselves. What’s likely, however, is that even if dedicated AI testers exist, the functional consultant will still need to closely cooperate and provide business context – so some level of understanding of the new testing paradigms is necessary.

Regardless of the approach, one thing is clear: the need for robust testing of end-to-end processes (including AI actions) is only going to increase. SAP functional consultants are in a prime position to take up that challenge, given their deep understanding of business rules and process outcomes. They will not necessarily code test scripts from scratch, but they will orchestrate and validate tests using new-generation tools. As the enterprise software world embraces “probabilistic” AI agents and highly API-driven architectures, the consultant’s role evolves from simply configuring and eyeballing the result on a screen, to actively safeguarding the integrity of complex, automated workflows.

Preparing for the Future

For SAP functional consultants wondering how to stay ahead in this changing landscape, here are a few final thoughts:

  • Get Familiar with Integration Technologies: You don’t need to be a developer, but learn the basics of how data enters/exits SAP in your domain (OData services, IDoc flows, BAPIs, etc.). Understand the tools available (e.g., SAP API Hub, Int4 Suite, or SAP’s own testing utilities) to send or monitor those interfaces.
  • Experiment with AI (in a Safe Environment): If your organization is introducing SAP AI agents like Joule, try to get involved in the early testing. Ask to be part of the pilot. Treat it as an opportunity to build “AI literacy” – learn how the agent is supposed to operate, what data it uses, and how to trigger it. Maybe set up a sandbox where you can play with the agent’s skills without risk.
  • Leverage Test Automation Tools Designed for SAP: Tools such as Int4 Suite (and others in the SAP ecosystem) are there to help. They are built with SAP processes in mind (understanding IDocs, BAPIs, document flows) which generic testing tools might not easily handle. Investing time to learn one of these tools can pay off greatly. As we saw, providing simulation agents to consultants can change the game by eliminating dependencies and speeding up tests. It’s worth advocating within your project to bring in such tools – not just for AI, but for any integrated process testing.
  • Mindset Shift – From Tester to Guardian of the Process: Finally, embrace a mindset change. Instead of seeing testing as a step where you click through a script to confirm your configuration works, view yourself as the guardian of the end-to-end process. Your goal is to ensure that when all the moving parts (SAP modules, integrations, AI agents, partner systems) play together, the business outcome is correct and reliable. This holistic view will naturally drive you to adopt whatever techniques and tools are needed (be it learning to read a JSON payload or interpreting an AI agent log). It’s an exciting expansion of the functional consultant’s role – one that can make you even more valuable in an AI-powered enterprise.

Conclusion

Yes, SAP functional consultants will evolve to become more like AI/API testers for their business processes – in fact, they will become full-spectrum process validators. They’ll still use their core business knowledge, but augmented with new testing skills and tools. The introduction of AI agents and heavily integrated processes doesn’t diminish the importance of the functional consultant; on the contrary, it amplifies the need for their insight to ensure these intelligent systems do the right thing. The likely scenario (and in many cases, the only viable one) is that consultants embrace this change: using specialized tools, they will test conversations and API calls with the same comfort as they tested transactions in SAPGUI. This comprehensive approach is what will maintain enterprise-grade trust in an era where software is no longer fully deterministic. By adopting solutions that allow deep backend verification and service virtualization, functional consultants can close the “verification gap” and keep their processes running smoothly.

Further Learning on Int4 Suite 

  • SAP Learning Course: Avoid SAP S/4HANA Project Delays with Third-Party Systems Service Virtualization

entry.title: Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Welcome to your one-stop shop to learn and share knowledge about SAP's ready-to-run public cloud ERP software solution.  

SAP Cloud ERP is part of SAP Business Suite that brings AI, data, and applications together into a fully integrated system. This community is open for anyone in the Cloud ERP ecosystem–customers, partners, and SAP experts.  

 

In this blog post I’d like to outline the innovations for SAP Cloud ERP 2602 in the area of Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management. These are the topics that are covered:

  • Enhanced integration of project plan information with SAP Cloud ERP (6JN) 
  • Project staffing view for resource managers (6JO) 
  • Allowing contingent workers (cost center procured) to record time on enterprise projects (1NT, 1Q4) 
  • Automatic removal of billing block for sales order billing plans (1NT) 
  • Joule as project assistant (beta), planned to be available mid-March
  • Further innovations

 

Check out my video to get a glimpse of the new features:

 

In today's fast-paced business environment, running projects efficiently, on time, and on budget is more critical than ever. This release is packed with features designed to bring more intelligence, automation, and transparency to your projects, ultimately driving significant business value. We’ve focused on streamlining processes, enhancing integration, and even giving you a glimpse into the future with AI-powered assistance.

Let's dive into the highlights that will help you unlock new levels of project success.

 

Enhancements in SAP Project and Resource Management

 

Enhanced integration of project plan information with SAP Cloud ERP

In complex projects spanning engineering, manufacturing, and services, a major challenge is keeping project execution plans in sync with core financial and logistical data. A delay in a design phase, for example, has a direct impact on procurement timelines and billing dates. If this information isn't shared instantly between the project planning tool and the core ERP, decisions are made based on outdated information. To solve this, our 2602 release supercharges the integration between SAP Project and Resource Management, our solution on the SAP Business Technology Platform, and SAP Cloud ERP. Now, when a project manager adjusts a project plan – such as extending a phase – the system automatically synchronizes these date changes with the core ERP in real time.

This direct synchronization ensures that your financial, commercial, and logistical data within SAP Cloud ERP always reflects the most current project reality, empowering you to make reliable decisions based on a single source of truth. By automating the update process, you eliminate the need for project teams to manually replicate date changes across systems, which significantly reduces administrative effort and minimizes the risk of human error. This tight integration also lays the groundwork for faster billing cycles, as milestone completions in the project plan can now directly trigger commercial processes in the core system.

 

Figure 1: Improved integration of project plan information between SAP Cloud ERP and SAP Project and Resource Management, enables better decision-making in projects through reliable, up-to-date project plan information.

 

Project staffing view for resource managers

Resource managers are constantly juggling demands from multiple projects, and gaining a clear, consolidated overview of the staffing situation for a specific project can be a fragmented and time-consuming process. To address this pain point, we are introducing a brand-new SAP Fiori app called “Manage Request Assignments”. This powerful application is designed specifically for resource managers, providing a single, project-centric view of all resource requests. It shows at a glance which roles have been fully staffed, which are partially staffed, and which are still open, eliminating the need to dig through multiple screens and reports.

This new application provides resource managers with unprecedented transparency into the staffing health of any project, allowing them to instantly identify bottlenecks and open demands without extensive research. Having all the necessary information in one place, with direct navigation to take action, streamlines the workflow and dramatically reduces the time it takes to staff open requests and get the right people on the right tasks. Ultimately, a clear, holistic overview empowers resource managers to more effectively optimize staffing within a single project or re-allocate experts across multiple projects, ensuring your most valuable resources are deployed with maximum impact.

 

Figure 2: The “Manage Request Assignments” application allows users to efficiently view the resource request and assignment details at a project level.

 

Other Innovations in SAP Project and Resource Management

Additional enhancements introduced in SAP Project and Resource Management. For example:

  • Technical user flow coverage (client credential flow) for some APIs
  • Use of actual dates in scheduling
  • Consideration of working and none working days in scheduling
  • Ability to block certain file types during file upload
  • Additional details for tasks and collaboration project

For a complete list and more details please take a look into the What's new document for SAP Project and Resource Management

 

 

Enhancements in Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management

 

Allowing contingent workers (cost center procured) to record time on enterprise projects

Modern project teams are a dynamic blend of internal employees and external contingent workers. A significant process gap existed for contingent workers who were procured via a cost center, as they couldn't use the standard “My Timesheet” application to record time against enterprise projects. This made it difficult to get a complete and timely picture of all labor hours and costs. With the 2602 release, we have closed this gap. Now, these contingent workers can use the very same “My Timesheet” application as internal employees to record their time against the projects they are staffed on, creating a unified experience for the entire team.

This enhancement delivers a unified and simplified process where all project resources, regardless of their employment type, use a single application for time recording, which improves the user experience. You can now capture every hour worked against your project demands in one central place, providing a more accurate and timely view of total project costs and progress. Furthermore, the system automates the creation of the corresponding service entry sheet and goods receipt upon timesheet approval, significantly reducing administrative overhead and ensuring your suppliers are paid accurately and on time.

 

Figure 3: Project-based and cost center-based contingent workers can record time seamlessly in the “Manage My Timesheet” application, ensuring consistent tracking and better visibility across all engagement types.

 

Automatic removal of billing block for sales order billing plans

Milestone billing is a fantastic way to align payments with project progress, but it has often been hampered by a manual step. Once a milestone was achieved, someone had to go into the sales order and manually remove the billing block to make that item available for invoicing, a delay that directly impacts cash flow. We have now fully automated this process. Building on a feature from our last release, the system now automatically removes the billing block from the corresponding billing plan item in the related sales order the moment a project manager confirms the completion of that milestone in the project.

By completely automating this process, you eliminate the manual hand-off and inherent delays, allowing you to invoice your customers the very moment a milestone is contractually met. This directly accelerates your order-to-cash cycle and improves cash flow. This automation also removes a tedious and error-prone manual task, freeing up your project controllers and sales administrators to focus their expertise on more strategic, value-added activities. The risk of human error is entirely eliminated, leading to a smoother, more reliable, and more accurate billing process from end to end.

 

Figure 4: For sales order billing plan items which are created based on enterprise project or WBS element milestones, the billing block is automatically removed when the related milestone is completed.

 

Joule as project assistant (beta), planned to be available mid-March

Setting up a new project can be a complex and time-consuming task, often requiring project managers to navigate between three or four different applications to define the scope, staff resources, and release the project for time recording. We are incredibly excited to give you a preview of how we are solving this with the future of project management: the Joule Project Assistant. This is our generative AI copilot, and we are starting with a beta agent designed to radically simplify the creation of internal projects. Instead of clicking through multiple screens, a project manager can simply ask Joule in natural language, e.g. "Create a new pre-sales project for the 'Rising Star' initiative, starting next week and lasting for one month.". Joule can then propose a template, adjust dates, staff the project, and release it – all within a single, conversational session.

This AI-powered assistant promises dramatic efficiency gains, condensing a multi-application process into a single, swift conversation that saves significant time and effort for your project managers. The intuitive, conversational nature of Joule makes project creation accessible to everyone, drastically reducing the learning curve and empowering users to initiate projects quickly and correctly. By leveraging templates from successful past projects, the Joule agent also helps ensure that new projects are set up consistently and in compliance with your company's standards right from the start. We are currently running a beta program for this and encourage you to get involved! Register for the Customer Engagement Initiative via this landing page as of March 15, 2026.

 

Figure 5: The AI-powered solution assists users in rapidly setting up projects by leveraging similar past projects when the volume is high, and projects are repetitive in nature.



Other innovations in Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management
